evals.prompt: Find breaking API changes in this project
evals.description: Express.js app with routes split across multiple router files. The main app.js mounts routers at different prefixes. One router's mount prefix changes (/users -> /api/v2/users), causing all its routes to move. Another router removes one endpoint and adds a new one. The skill must reconstruct full paths by combining app.use() prefix with route-level paths.
evals.expected_output: GET /users, GET /users/:id, POST /users, DELETE /users/:id all flagged as breaking path changes (moved to /api/v2/users prefix). DELETE /orders/:id flagged as removed (breaking). PATCH /orders/:id flagged as new non-breaking addition. The skill must trace the prefix from app.js to determine full paths.

assertions.text: The path change from /users to /api/v2/users is flagged as breaking (all user routes moved)
DELETE /orders/:id is flagged as a breaking change because the endpoint was removed
PATCH /orders/:id is listed as a non-breaking addition
The report identifies that the full path was determined by combining the app.use() prefix with the route-level path (i.e. it found the prefix in app.js, not just the route file)

evals.description: FastAPI app where a shared Pydantic response model is used by three endpoints. A field is removed from the shared model (breaking all three), and a new required field is added to a request model for one endpoint. A new optional field is also added to the shared response model. The skill must identify that the shared model change cascades to all endpoints that use it.
evals.expected_output: eta_seconds removed from JobResponse is breaking for ALL THREE endpoints (POST /jobs, GET /jobs/{job_id}, GET /jobs/{job_id}/status) because they all use JobResponse as response_model. queue_position added is non-breaking (new field). tenant_id added as required field to CreateJobRequest is breaking for POST /jobs (clients that omit it will get 422). The report must connect the shared model change to all affected endpoints.

assertions.text: The removal of eta_seconds from JobResponse is flagged as breaking and the report identifies that it affects all three endpoints (POST /jobs, GET /jobs/{id}, GET /jobs/{id}/status)
The addition of queue_position to JobResponse is listed as non-breaking (new optional response field)
The addition of tenant_id as a required field in CreateJobRequest is flagged as breaking for POST /jobs (clients that omit it will get a 422 validation error)
The report identifies that the breaking change originates from a shared model (JobResponse in models.py) rather than listing each endpoint independently without explanation

evals.prompt: What breaking changes are there in this API?
evals.description: Spring Boot REST controller where the class-level @RequestMapping sets the base path. A method's path changes, a method gets a required request parameter added, and auth is added to one previously open endpoint via @PreAuthorize. The full path must be assembled from class + method annotations.
evals.expected_output: All endpoints at /api/users/* are breaking path changes because the class-level @RequestMapping changed from /api/users to /api/v2/users. GET /api/users/export renamed to GET /api/v2/users/reports (path changed + endpoint effectively removed). GET /api/v2/users/reports requires ADMIN role (auth added, breaking for unauthenticated clients) and a required 'format' param (breaking for clients not sending it). The skill must combine class-level and method-level annotations for full paths.

assertions.text: The change to the class-level @RequestMapping (from /api/users to /api/v2/users) is identified as the source of all path changes, affecting every endpoint in the controller
The rename of /export to /reports is flagged as a breaking path change (GET /api/users/export is gone)
The addition of @PreAuthorize('hasRole(ADMIN)') to the reports endpoint is flagged as a breaking change (previously open endpoint now requires ADMIN role)
The addition of the required 'format' request parameter to GET /api/v2/users/reports is flagged as breaking

evals.prompt: Find breaking changes in this API
evals.description: Flask app using Blueprints. A blueprint's url_prefix changes at registration time (in app.py), and within the blueprint a route's HTTP method changes (GET -> POST) and a response field is removed. The skill must trace the blueprint prefix from the registration call, not from the route decorators themselves.
evals.expected_output: All reports endpoints change path: GET /reports/summary -> GET /api/v2/reports/summary (breaking), GET /reports/export -> POST /api/v2/reports/export (two breaks: path changed AND method changed from GET to POST). Response field 'generated_at' removed from GET /api/v2/reports/summary (breaking). Payments endpoints are unchanged. The skill must identify that the prefix change came from app/__init__.py registration, not from the route decorators.

assertions.text: The report identifies that the path change originated from the url_prefix change in the blueprint registration (app/__init__.py), not from the route decorators
The path change from /reports/* to /api/v2/reports/* is flagged as breaking for all report endpoints
GET /reports/export changing to POST /api/v2/reports/export is flagged as breaking — both the path AND the HTTP method changed
The removal of the 'generated_at' field from the GET /reports/summary response is flagged as a breaking change
The payments endpoints are correctly identified as unchanged and do not appear as breaking changes

evals.prompt: Are there breaking changes in this API?
evals.description: OpenAPI spec (YAML) where a shared component schema ($ref) is modified. The schema 'ErrorResponse' is used in error responses for multiple endpoints. A required field is added to it, and one endpoint's success response schema loses a field. A completely new endpoint is also added. The skill must follow $ref pointers to determine which endpoints are affected by shared schema changes.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-rest-4 && cd /tmp/eval-rest-4 && git init && cat > openapi.yaml << 'EOF'
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Orders API
  version: 1.0.0
components:
  schemas:
    ErrorResponse:
      type: object
      required: [code, message]
      properties:
        code:
          type: string
        message:
          type: string
    Order:
      type: object
      required: [id, status, total]
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
        total:
          type: number
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
paths:
  /orders:
    get:
      summary: List orders
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Success
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
        '400':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    post:
      summary: Create order
      responses:
        '201':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
        '400':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
  /orders/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get order
      responses:
        '200':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
        '404':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
EOF
git add . && git commit -m 'v1.0.0' && git tag v1.0.0
cat > openapi.yaml << 'EOF'
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Orders API
  version: 2.0.0
components:
  schemas:
    ErrorResponse:
      type: object
      required: [code, message, request_id]
      properties:
        code:
          type: string
        message:
          type: string
        request_id:
          type: string
    Order:
      type: object
      required: [id, status, total]
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
        total:
          type: number
paths:
  /orders:
    get:
      summary: List orders
      responses:
        '200':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
        '400':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    post:
      summary: Create order
      responses:
        '201':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
        '400':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
  /orders/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get order
      responses:
        '200':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
        '404':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
  /orders/{id}/cancel:
    post:
      summary: Cancel order
      responses:
        '200':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Order'
EOF
git add . && git commit -m 'add request_id to ErrorResponse, remove created_at from Order, add cancel endpoint'
evals.expected_output: ErrorResponse gains required field 'request_id' — this is breaking for ALL endpoints that return ErrorResponse (GET /orders, POST /orders, GET /orders/{id}), because clients parsing error responses will now receive an unexpected required field they may not handle. Order schema loses 'created_at' — this is breaking for GET /orders, POST /orders, GET /orders/{id} since all return Order. POST /orders/{id}/cancel is a new non-breaking endpoint. The skill must follow $ref pointers.

assertions.text: The addition of required 'request_id' to ErrorResponse is identified as affecting multiple endpoints (GET /orders, POST /orders, GET /orders/{id}) because they all reference ErrorResponse via $ref
The removal of 'created_at' from the Order schema is flagged as a breaking change affecting endpoints that return Order objects
POST /orders/{id}/cancel is listed as a non-breaking addition
The report demonstrates awareness of $ref resolution — it connects schema changes in components/schemas to the endpoints that use those schemas via $ref

evals.prompt: Find breaking API changes in this Spring project
evals.description: Spring Boot app with a three-level path composition: a base controller class has @RequestMapping('/api/v1'), a child controller extends it with @RequestMapping('/users'), and individual methods have @GetMapping('/{id}') etc. The base class path changes from /api/v1 to /api/v2. The skill must trace the inheritance chain to know that the full path is base-class-prefix + controller-prefix + method-path.
evals.expected_output: ALL endpoints in both UsersController and OrdersController are breaking path changes because the base class @RequestMapping changed from /api/v1 to /api/v2. Full paths change from /api/v1/users/* to /api/v2/users/* and from /api/v1/orders/* to /api/v2/orders/*. The skill must trace the inheritance chain: UsersController extends BaseApiController, BaseApiController has @RequestMapping('/api/v1'). The report should attribute the root cause to BaseApiController.java, not just list each endpoint independently.

assertions.text: The change to @RequestMapping in BaseApiController (from /api/v1 to /api/v2) is identified as the root cause affecting all controllers that extend it
All UsersController endpoints are flagged as breaking path changes (/api/v1/users/* → /api/v2/users/*)
All OrdersController endpoints are flagged as breaking path changes (/api/v1/orders/* → /api/v2/orders/*)

The report identifies that full paths were computed by combining the base class prefix with the controller-level and method-level mappings (i.e. it read BaseApiController.java, not just the child controllers)

evals.description: Spring Boot app where auth rules live entirely in a separate SecurityConfig.java (WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter). The controller files do not change at all — only SecurityConfig changes. An endpoint that was previously open (permitAll) now requires ADMIN role. A second endpoint that required USER role is now open. The skill must read the security config to determine auth requirements; the breaking change is invisible if you only look at controller annotations.
evals.expected_output: POST /api/products is a breaking auth change: was USER, now requires ADMIN (clients with USER role will get 403). GET /api/products/internal/stats is a breaking auth change in reverse direction: was USER-only, now permitAll — this opens a previously restricted endpoint which may be a security concern but is non-breaking for existing clients. Controller files unchanged. The skill must trace auth rules from SecurityConfig.java and match them to endpoints in ProductController.java.

assertions.text: POST /api/products is flagged as a breaking auth change — the required role escalated from USER to ADMIN, so existing USER clients will get 403
GET /api/products/internal/stats is noted as an auth change — the endpoint changed from USER-required to open (permitAll)
The report attributes the auth changes to SecurityConfig.java, not to controller annotations (the controller was not modified)
GET /api/products (list) and DELETE /api/products/{id} are correctly identified as unchanged

evals.prompt: What breaking changes are there in this API?
evals.description: Spring Boot app where all controllers wrap their responses in a generic ApiResponse<T> class. A field is removed from ApiResponse<T> itself (requestId), which cascades to every endpoint across multiple controllers. Additionally, a new required constraint (@NotNull) is added to a field in CreateOrderRequest, making that POST endpoint breaking for clients that omit the field.
evals.expected_output: requestId removed from ApiResponse<T> is a breaking response change that cascades to ALL four endpoints: GET /api/users/{id}, GET /api/users, GET /api/orders/{id}, POST /api/orders. The skill must recognize that ApiResponse<T> is a generic wrapper used by all controllers and trace the cascade. Additionally, @NotNull added to customerId in CreateOrderRequest is breaking for POST /api/orders — clients that omit customerId will now get a 400 validation error.

assertions.text: The removal of requestId from ApiResponse<T> is flagged as breaking and identified as affecting all four endpoints across both controllers (GET /api/users/{id}, GET /api/users, GET /api/orders/{id}, POST /api/orders)

The report identifies that the breaking change originates from the shared generic wrapper class ApiResponse.java and cascades to all endpoints that use it, rather than listing each endpoint as an independent change
The addition of @NotNull to customerId in CreateOrderRequest is flagged as a breaking change for POST /api/orders

evals.prompt: Find breaking changes in this API
evals.description: Spring Boot app where breaking changes are expressed entirely through Bean Validation constraint annotations on a DTO — no route or method signatures change. @NotNull and @Email are added to fields that previously had no constraints. @Size min increases. These manifest as HTTP 400 errors for previously-valid client payloads. The skill must recognize that constraint annotation changes are API-breaking changes.
evals.expected_output: POST /api/users/register has multiple breaking constraint changes: email is now @NotNull (clients that omit email will get 400 — previously valid), username @Size min increased from 2 to 5 (clients sending 2-4 char usernames now get 400), password @Size min increased from 6 to 8 (clients sending 6-7 char passwords now get 400). PUT /api/users/{id}/profile changes are non-breaking (only @Size max added, which is more permissive or same). The skill must recognize that tightening validation constraints is an API-breaking change.

assertions.text: The addition of @NotNull/@Email to email in RegisterRequest is flagged as breaking for POST /api/users/register — clients not sending email will now receive 400
The increase of @Size min on username from 2 to 5 is flagged as breaking — previously valid 2-4 character usernames are now rejected
The increase of @Size min on password from 6 to 8 is flagged as breaking — previously valid 6-7 character passwords are now rejected
The changes to UpdateProfileRequest (adding @Size max constraints) are correctly identified as non-breaking additions

evals.prompt: Are there breaking API changes in this project?
evals.description: Spring Boot app where a @ControllerAdvice GlobalExceptionHandler handles all validation errors and not-found errors across every endpoint. The error response format changes entirely (different field names and structure) in the exception handler. No controller files change. The skill must recognize that @ControllerAdvice changes affect the error contract of all endpoints, even though no route files were touched.
evals.expected_output: The error response format changed in GlobalExceptionHandler.java (a @ControllerAdvice that handles errors for all endpoints). Old format: {errors: ["field: message"]}. New format: {code: string, violations: [{field, message}], timestamp: string}. This is a breaking change affecting ALL endpoints that return validation errors (POST /api/items, PUT /api/items/{id}) because clients parsing the 400 response body will break. Also affects all endpoints returning 404 (GET /api/items/{id}, DELETE /api/items/{id}) since the not-found error format changed. ItemController was not modified.

assertions.text: The change to the error response format in GlobalExceptionHandler is flagged as a breaking change — the errors[] array format was replaced by violations[{field,message}] with a code field
POST /api/items and PUT /api/items/{id} are identified as affected by the validation error format change (they accept @Valid request bodies)
GET /api/items/{id} and DELETE /api/items/{id} are identified as affected by the not-found error format change
The report identifies that the change originates from @ControllerAdvice (GlobalExceptionHandler.java) and that this affects all endpoints, not just one — the controller file itself was not modified

evals.prompt: Find breaking API changes in this project
evals.description: FastAPI app where a shared Pydantic response model (ProductResponse) loses a field. Two router files that use ProductResponse as response_model are NOT changed — they don't appear in the git diff at all. The skill must proactively search for usages of the changed schema in unchanged files to identify which endpoints are affected. Baseline only sees the schema file in the diff.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-rest-10/app/routers && cd /tmp/eval-rest-10 && git init && cat > app/schemas.py << 'EOF'
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional

class ProductResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    name: str
    price: float
    cost_price: float
    stock: int
    sku: Optional[str] = None

class CreateProductRequest(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
    cost_price: float
    sku: Optional[str] = None
EOF
cat > app/routers/products.py << 'EOF'
from fastapi import APIRouter
from typing import List
from ..schemas import ProductResponse, CreateProductRequest

router = APIRouter()

@router.get('/', response_model=List[ProductResponse])
def list_products():
    return []

@router.get('/{product_id}', response_model=ProductResponse)
def get_product(product_id: str):
    return {}

@router.post('/', response_model=ProductResponse)
def create_product(req: CreateProductRequest):
    return {}
EOF
cat > app/routers/catalog.py << 'EOF'
from fastapi import APIRouter
from typing import List
from ..schemas import ProductResponse

router = APIRouter()

@router.get('/featured', response_model=List[ProductResponse])
def get_featured():
    return []

@router.get('/new-arrivals', response_model=List[ProductResponse])
def get_new_arrivals():
    return []
EOF
cat > app/main.py << 'EOF'
from fastapi import FastAPI
from .routers import products, catalog

app = FastAPI()
app.include_router(products.router, prefix='/api/products', tags=['products'])
app.include_router(catalog.router, prefix='/api/catalog', tags=['catalog'])
EOF
git add . && git commit -m 'v1.0.0' && git tag v1.0.0
cat > app/schemas.py << 'EOF'
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional

class ProductResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    name: str
    price: float
    stock: int
    sku: Optional[str] = None

class CreateProductRequest(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
    sku: Optional[str] = None
EOF
git add . && git commit -m 'remove cost_price from ProductResponse and CreateProductRequest (internal field, should not be exposed)'
evals.expected_output: cost_price removed from ProductResponse is a breaking response change affecting ALL endpoints that return ProductResponse: GET /api/products, GET /api/products/{product_id}, POST /api/products (from routers/products.py) AND GET /api/catalog/featured, GET /api/catalog/new-arrivals (from routers/catalog.py). cost_price removed from CreateProductRequest is a breaking request change for POST /api/products. The router files were NOT in the diff — the skill must grep for ProductResponse/CreateProductRequest usages to find the affected endpoints.

assertions.text: The removal of cost_price from ProductResponse is flagged as a breaking response change
GET /api/products, GET /api/products/{product_id}, and POST /api/products are all identified as affected (from routers/products.py which was NOT in the diff)
GET /api/catalog/featured and GET /api/catalog/new-arrivals are identified as affected (from routers/catalog.py which was NOT in the diff)

The report identifies that the router files (products.py, catalog.py) were not modified but are affected because they use ProductResponse as response_model — the skill traced usages in unchanged files

evals.description: Spring Boot app where a @RequestBody DTO gains a required field. Two controller files that accept this DTO as @RequestBody are NOT changed and do not appear in the git diff. The skill must grep for usages of the changed DTO class in unchanged controller files to identify which POST endpoints now require the new field.
evals.expected_output: sourceChannel added as required (@NotBlank) to CreateOrderRequest is a breaking change for POST /api/orders (OrderController) AND POST /api/admin/orders (AdminOrderController). Both controllers accept CreateOrderRequest as @RequestBody with @Valid. Neither controller file changed — only CreateOrderRequest.java is in the diff. The skill must grep for CreateOrderRequest usages in the controller files to find both affected endpoints.

assertions.text: The addition of required field sourceChannel (@NotBlank) to CreateOrderRequest is flagged as a breaking change — clients not sending it will receive 400
POST /api/orders from OrderController is identified as affected (OrderController.java was NOT in the diff)
POST /api/admin/orders from AdminOrderController is identified as affected (AdminOrderController.java was NOT in the diff)
The report correctly notes that only CreateOrderRequest.java was modified and that the controller files were unchanged — the breaking change was discovered by tracing DTO usage in unchanged files

evals.prompt: Check this repo for breaking API changes
evals.description: Flask app where a shared marshmallow Schema (UserSchema) loses a field. Three blueprint view files that use UserSchema to serialize responses are NOT in the diff. The skill must search for UserSchema usages in the blueprint files to enumerate all affected endpoints. Additionally a second schema (TokenSchema) gains a required field, affecting an auth endpoint whose file is also unchanged.
evals.expected_output: phone removed from UserSchema is breaking for ALL endpoints that return UserSchema: GET /api/users, GET /api/users/{user_id}, POST /api/users (from users.py — NOT in diff) and GET /api/profile/me, PUT /api/profile/me (from profile.py — NOT in diff). refresh_token added as required to TokenSchema is breaking for POST /api/auth/login and POST /api/auth/refresh (from auth.py — NOT in diff). Only schemas.py is in the diff. The skill must search for UserSchema and TokenSchema usages in blueprint files to enumerate all 7 affected endpoints.

assertions.text: The removal of phone from UserSchema is flagged as a breaking response change
GET /api/users, GET /api/users/{user_id}, and POST /api/users from users.py are identified as affected by the UserSchema change (users.py was NOT in the diff)
GET /api/profile/me and PUT /api/profile/me from profile.py are identified as affected by the UserSchema change (profile.py was NOT in the diff)
POST /api/auth/login and POST /api/auth/refresh are identified as affected by the required refresh_token addition to TokenSchema (auth.py was NOT in the diff)
